task: Review whether the agent explored enough evidence before applying a red-block corner rule.
observations: Training example A maps a red block to the top-left corner.
Training example B maps a red triangle to the top-left corner, so shape is not the deciding factor.
Training example C maps a large red block to the top-left corner, so size is not the deciding factor.
Training example D keeps object order unchanged while moving the red object to the top-left corner.
hypothesis: Red objects move to the top-left corner independent of shape, size, or object order.
actions: Test whether shape changes the transformation.
Test whether size or object order changes the transformation.
final_answer: Move the red block to the top-left corner.
